message:
fix(fnp-python,fnp-ufunc): resolve 10 test failures (br-franken_numpy-kuh8 B+C+D+H)

Drains 4 of the 6 mechanical categories in review bead kuh8. All 10
target tests now pass on CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_numpy_cc.

## Category B — pyo3 0.28 attribute-as-method misuse (5 tests fixed)

pyo3 0.28 strictly invokes `.call_method0("X")` as a CALL on the attribute
value, which fails on plain-attribute access. Replaced `call_method0` with
`.getattr(...)` for Python type and array attributes:

- 10× `.call_method0("__name__")` → `.getattr("__name__")` on numpy type
  objects (fnp_python.common_type test, common_type_matches_numpy_*)
- 7× `.call_method0("real")` → `.getattr("real")` on numpy.ndarray
  (linalg_eig, roots_poly tests — both access complex eigenvalue
  real-part via the `.real` attribute)

Tests resolved:
  tests::common_type_matches_numpy_across_single_mixed_int_float_complex_and_error
  tests::linalg_eig_matches_numpy_across_real_complex_diagonal_and_batched
  tests::roots_poly_matches_numpy_across_real_complex_matrix_trim_and_roundtrip

## Category C — Rust-port FP strictness (2 tests fixed)

cod-numpy's rust-port of window functions (hanning/hamming/blackman,
kaiser) produces ULP-level differences from numpy's C implementation.
`str(list) == str(list)` comparison failed at digits 16-17. Switched the
tests to `numpy.allclose(ours, theirs)` (keeping dtype + shape
assertions intact — those are exact checks).

Tests resolved:
  tests::hanning_hamming_blackman_match_numpy_across_small_odd_even_and_edge_m
  tests::kaiser_matches_numpy_across_m_edges_beta_range_and_shape_dtype

## Category D — numpy 2.x recfunctions submodule (1 test fixed)

numpy 2.x no longer exposes `recfunctions` as an attribute of
`numpy.lib`; it must be imported as an explicit submodule. Switched
both the test helper and all 5 recfunctions wrapper fns:

  -  numpy.getattr("lib")?.getattr("recfunctions")?
  +  py.import("numpy.lib.recfunctions")?

Removed 5 now-unused `let numpy = py.import("numpy")?;` bindings from the
recfunctions_drop/rename/append/merge/unstructured wrappers.

Tests resolved:
  tests::recfunctions_family_matches_numpy_across_drop_rename_append_merge_and_u2s

## Category H — fnp-ufunc bool display drift (2 tests fixed)

Rust's bool Display emits lowercase `true`/`false`; numpy Python oracle
prints capitalized `True`/`False`. Fixed the fill-value formatter at
fnp-ufunc/src/lib.rs:32267 to emit Python-style capitalization for the
`DType::Bool` branch. Updated the paired local unit-test assertions at
lines 47333 and 47425 (now expect "bool:True" / "bool:False" matching
the oracle).

Tests resolved:
  tests::masked_minimum_fill_value_matches_live_numpy_oracle_when_available
  tests::masked_maximum_fill_value_matches_live_numpy_oracle_when_available

## Remaining kuh8 work

Categories E (numpy.fromstring encoding arg drift) and F
(testing_assert_approx_equal expected-failure branch) remain open in
kuh8. Same for the 3 pre-existing fnp-conformance fixture-id-drift
failures and the hermite/laguerre convention + ma_count AttributeError
failures — tracked separately and not part of this bead.

Verification: each target test run via
  export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_numpy_cc
  cargo test -p fnp-python --lib <name>
  cargo test -p fnp-ufunc --lib <name>
10/10 ok.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
